articles.title: Actor Alexander Morton from TV Show Dies at 81
articles.summary: Alexander Morton, an actor from Scotland, has died at 81 years old. He was famous for playing Golly Mackenzie in the TV series Monarch of the Glen, which is a show about a big estate in the Highlands. He acted in all 64 episodes from 2000 to 2005. Morton also played other roles in movies and TV shows like Get Carter and Take The High Road. He started acting in the 1970s and worked for many years on stage and screen. People from BBC Scotland and his friend Robert Carlyle said they are sad and remember him as a kind and talented actor who helped make Scottish TV special. He will be missed by fans and friends.

articles.title: Joy Harmon: A Famous Actress from an Old Movie
articles.summary: Joy Harmon was an actress who lived to be 87 years old. She is best known for a short scene in the movie Cool Hand Luke, where she washes a car. In this scene, she uses a sponge and soap, and prisoners watch her. She did not speak in the movie, but her character was called Lucille. Joy started as a child model and was in many TV shows and movies from the 1950s to 1970s. After acting, she worked at Disney and opened a bakery. She had three children and nine grandchildren. She died at home in Los Angeles after being sick with pneumonia.

articles.title: Live Nation Found Guilty of Being a Monopoly and Overcharging Fans
articles.summary: A big company called Live Nation, which owns Ticketmaster, was found guilty by a jury of being a monopoly and charging fans too much for tickets. This happened after a trial in New York City that lasted seven weeks. The company might have to sell parts of its business or split from Ticketmaster because of this. The US government said Live Nation's actions stopped other companies from competing, which made ticket prices higher and service worse. Live Nation disagrees and says the verdict isn't final yet. This case could help smaller ticket-sellers and venues compete better, which might lower ticket prices and help more performers get shows. Experts say this is a big win for fans and artists, and it warns other big companies. Live Nation's stock price dropped after the news.

articles.title: Jamie Murray Says Goodbye to Tennis
articles.summary: Jamie Murray, a famous tennis player from Britain, has decided to stop playing tennis. He is 40 years old and has been playing for 36 years. Jamie won seven big tournaments called Grand Slams and was the best doubles player in the world. He played with his brother Andy, and together they helped Britain win the Davis Cup in 2015 for the first time in 79 years. Jamie says he feels lucky for all the fun experiences in tennis and is excited to try new things in the real world. He thanked his family and friends for their support. Jamie last played at the US Open in August and now wants to move on to a different life.

articles.title: Jamie Murray Says Goodbye to Tennis
articles.summary: Jamie Murray, a famous tennis player, has decided to stop playing tennis. He is 40 years old and has won seven big tournaments called Grand Slams. He said it was a tough year for him, and he has not played since last August. Now, he is excited to start a new life outside of tennis. This news comes from the BBC, a trusted news source. Jamie Murray is known for being a great doubles player and has had a long career. His retirement means he will no longer compete in tennis matches, but fans will remember his achievements.

articles.title: Madonna's New Dance Album
articles.summary: Madonna is making a new album called Confessions II, which is a follow-up to her 2005 album Confessions On A Dancefloor. She announced it on Instagram by changing her bio to lyrics from her song Hung Up. The album will come out on July 3, and she shared a short video for the first track, I Feel So Free. Madonna worked with producer Stuart Price again, just like she did for the first album and her recent tour. She says dance music is important because it lets people express themselves without words. Madonna is 67 years old and this will be her 15th album. Her kids helped her record some parts too.
